old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.1","title":"Design Pair type system for Julia","description":"FeynCalc's Pair[x,y] encodes 3 things via argument heads: metric (LI,LI), four-vector (LI,Mom), scalar product (Mom,Mom). In Julia: decide whether to use one Pair type with canonical ordering + inner constructor (like FeynCalc) or separate types. Must handle Orderless (canonical arg ordering). Must integrate with TensorGR's TProduct. Research FeynCalc SharedObjects.m lines 1-300.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:41Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:41Z"}

new_value: Design spike complete: hybrid approach chosen (universal Pair type from FeynCalc + SPContext from Julia-idiomatic design + fold/reduce contract). Three agents ran: research, Design A, Design B.
Momentum type implemented in src/algebra/momentum.jl with DimSlot support, ordering, hash. 14 tests.
ScalarProduct mechanism implemented: Pair(Momentum,Momentum) replaces old ScalarProduct struct, SPContext for value storage, SP/SPD/SPE convenience constructors. 33 tests for Pair + 10 for SPContext.

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.5","title":"Implement Contract (Lorentz index contraction)","description":"Port FeynCalc Contract. THE core operation. Algorithm: FCSplit to separate indexed/free parts → DummyIndexFreeQ to filter → tree-walk to find repeated indices → substitute. In FeynCalc uses PairContract trick (UpValues for eager contraction). In Julia: explicit tree-walking pass via TensorGR's contract_metrics or a dedicated contract() function. Must handle: metric contraction g^μν g_νρ, four-vector contraction p^μ g_μν, BMHV mixed-dim contractions. Ref: Lorentz/Contract.m (718 lines), PairContract.m (427 lines).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z"}

new_value: Contract implemented in src/algebra/contract.jl: worklist reducer for metric-metric, metric-vector, vector-vector contractions. Full BMHV coverage (all 9x3 dim combinations). SPContext integration. Einstein violation detection. 46 contract tests.

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.4","title":"Implement ExpandScalarProduct","description":"Port FeynCalc ExpandScalarProduct: expand Pair[Momentum[a+b], Momentum[c]] → Pair[Momentum[a],Momentum[c]] + Pair[Momentum[b],Momentum[c]]. Uses linearity, NOT Mathematica's Expand. Finds tensor objects containing Plus and expands individually. Ref: Lorentz/ExpandScalarProduct.m (important: does NOT use Mathematica Expand).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z"}

new_value: ExpandScalarProduct implemented in expand_sp.jl. MomentumSum added to momentum.jl with full arithmetic. Bilinear expansion of Pair(MomentumSum,x), SPContext integration. 39 new tests.
